my_thoughts: The consequence test is set up: honor the "greatest power" claim, then show harm without knowledge. Expect intellectual resistance once harm is explicit; if they double down, use a concrete misuse case to force concession or reframe.
accumulated_insight: T1: Student calls rhetoric "the greatest of human powers" - cooperative engagement but firm value claim (affirms_value_claim). They understand: rhetoric = greatest power (value claim), power = supremacy (no scrutiny of use). The constraint_collapse mechanism tests: power without knowledge = can cause harm, forces choice between power and good use, threatens to collapse the "greatest" claim if knowledge is required. Pattern: bold claim + consequence test queued + [next: defense or concession]. Medium confidence because value claims often resist consequence framing, but pressing knowledge usually exposes the contradiction.
visible_response: Does he know what is truly good and what is bad?
